Exercise Name: "Plant your feet, arch your back slightly, and explode off the chest. Don't just move weight; move the floor."
"Touch the sky. Keep your elbows at 45 degrees to keep the fire in your upper chest, not your shoulders."
"Imagine hugging a massive tree. Squeeze your pinkies together at the center for that inner-chest split."
"Drive your elbows to your pockets. Pull your chest to the bar, not your chin."
"Keep your core like stone. Pull the bar to your belly button and crush an orange between your shoulder blades."
"Lean back slightly and pull with your elbows. Feel the stretch at the top, then rip it down."
"Head through the 'window' at the top. Lock out and dominate the weight over your center of gravity."
"Lead with your elbows and dump the water out of the pitchers. No swinging—pure shoulder isolation."
"Stop at eye level. Control the descent like a statue. Don't let gravity win on the way down."
"Chest up, knees out. Sit into the hole and drive through your heels like you're jumping through the ceiling."
"Don't lock your knees. Keep the tension on the quads and push the world away from you."
"Hinge at the hips. Push your glutes back until you feel the hamstrings scream, then snap back up."
"Hold the handles tight. Squeeze at the top for a full second. Make the quads burn."
"Keep your hips glued to the bench. Pull hard and resist the weight on the way back out."
"Deep stretch at the bottom, explosive pause at the top. Walk like a giant."
"Pinkies to the ceiling. No swinging. Turn the wrist at the top to peak the muscle."
"Crush the dumbbell handles. This is for thickness. Keep your elbows locked at your sides."
"Flare your hands at the bottom. Lock the elbows out and feel the horseshoe cramp."
"Deep stretch behind the head. Fire the weights to the ceiling and keep your elbows tucked in."
"Don't swing. Use your abs to pull your knees to your chest. Control is everything."
"Crunch into a ball. Pull with your abs, not your arms. Imagine touching your elbows to your knees."
"Follow your hands with your eyes. Rotate the whole torso, don't just move your arms."
